Class YamlDocument

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.block.Block<Map<Object,Block<?>>>
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.block.implementation.Section
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.YamlDocument
public class YamlDocument extends Section
Represents a YAML document.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
    protected
    YamlDocument(File document, InputStream defaults, Settings... settings)
    Creates and loads a YAML document from the given file and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream, if provided.
    protected
    YamlDocument(InputStream document, InputStream defaults, Settings... settings)
    Creates and loads a YAML document from the given stream and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream, if provided.

  • Constructor Details

    • YamlDocument

      protected YamlDocument(@NotNull InputStream document, @Nullable InputStream defaults, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given stream and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream, if provided.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      Please note that methods without an I/O parameter will not be usable. Refer to the method documentation for more information.

      Parameters:
      document - document
      defaults - defaults
      settings - settings
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • YamlDocument

      protected YamlDocument(@NotNull File document, @Nullable InputStream defaults, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given file and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream, if provided.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      If the given File does not exist, the document will be loaded from a copy of the defaults. If enabled, the file will automatically be created.

      Parameters:
      document - document (does not need to exist)
      defaults - defaults
      settings - settings
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

  • Method Details

    • reload

      public boolean reload() throws IOException
      Reloads the contents from the associated file using the associated loader and general settings.

      Returns if the operation was successful - false if there is no associated file, true otherwise.

      If the file does not exist, the document will be reloaded from a copy of the defaults (or empty if there are not any). Unless disabled, the file will automatically be created and saved.

      Returns:
      if the operation was successful (see getFile())
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • reload

      public void reload(@NotNull InputStream inputStream) throws IOException
      Reloads the contents from the given stream using the associated loader and general settings.

      If there is any associated file and it does not exist, unless disabled, the file will automatically be created and saved.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - file to reload from
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • reload

      public void reload(@NotNull InputStream inputStream, @NotNull LoaderSettings loaderSettings) throws IOException
      Reloads the contents from the given stream using the given loader and associated general settings.

      If there is any associated file and it does not exist, unless disabled, the file will automatically be created and saved.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - file to reload from
      loaderSettings - loader settings to use
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • update

      public boolean update() throws IOException
      Updates the contents against the associated defaults using the associated updater and general settings.

      Returns if the operation was successful - false if there are no defaults, true otherwise.

      Returns:
      if the operation was successful (see getDefaults())
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • update

      public boolean update(@NotNull UpdaterSettings updaterSettings) throws IOException
      Updates the contents against the associated defaults using the given updater and associated general settings.

      Returns if the operation was successful - false if there are no defaults, true otherwise.

      Parameters:
      updaterSettings - updater settings to use
      Returns:
      if the operation was successful (see getDefaults())
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • update

      public void update(@NotNull InputStream defaults) throws IOException
      Updates the contents against the given defaults using the associated updater and general settings.

      Please note that this involves loading a YAML document from the given stream. If you'd like to update, but don't want the defaults to be used in any other means, you can disable use of them via GeneralSettings.Builder.setUseDefaults(boolean) and provide the defaults right to the YamlDocument.create() method.

      Parameters:
      defaults - defaults to load and update against
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • update

      public void update(@NotNull InputStream defaults, @NotNull UpdaterSettings updaterSettings) throws IOException
      Updates the contents against the given defaults using the given updater and associated general settings.

      Please note that this involves loading a YAML document from the given stream. If you'd like to update, but don't want the defaults to be used in any other means, you can disable use of them via GeneralSettings.Builder.setUseDefaults(boolean) and provide the defaults right to the YamlDocument.create() method.

      Parameters:
      defaults - defaults to load and update against
      updaterSettings - updater settings to use
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • save

      public boolean save() throws IOException
      Saves the contents to the associated file using the associated dumper and general settings, in UTF-8 charset.

      Returns if the operation was successful - false if there is no associated file, true otherwise.

      Returns:
      if the operation was successful (see getFile())
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • save

      public void save(@NotNull File file) throws IOException
      Saves the contents to the given file using the associated dumper and general settings, in UTF-8 charset.
      Parameters:
      file - file to save to
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • save

      public void save(@NotNull OutputStream stream, Charset charset) throws IOException
      Saves the contents to the given stream using the associated dumper and general settings, in the given charset.
      Parameters:
      stream - stream to save to
      charset - charset to use
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • save

      public void save(@NotNull OutputStreamWriter writer) throws IOException
      Saves the contents to the given writer using the associated dumper and general settings.
      Parameters:
      writer - writer to save to
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • dump

      public String dump()
      Dumps the contents to a string using the associated dumper and general settings.
      Returns:
      the dumped contents

    • dump

      public String dump(@NotNull DumperSettings dumperSettings)
      Dumps the contents to a string using the given dumper and associated general settings.
      Parameters:
      dumperSettings - dumper settings to use
      Returns:
      the dumped contents

    • setSettings

      public void setSettings(@NotNull Settings... settings)
      Sets new settings to be used by this document, overwriting the previous settings associated with this document.

      If you are changing GeneralSettings:

      • It is required that the GeneralSettings.getKeyFormat() is the same as the one defined by getGeneralSettings() (settings currently in use by the document). Such attempt will result in an IllegalArgumentException; to update the key format, recreate the document.
      • Changing the default list, map or set suppliers will only affect collections created from now on.
      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.
      Parameters:
      settings - the new settings

    • setLoaderSettings

      @Deprecated public void setLoaderSettings(@NotNull LoaderSettings loaderSettings)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by setSettings(Settings...) and subject for removal
      Associates new loader settings.
      Parameters:
      loaderSettings - the new loader settings

    • setDumperSettings

      public void setDumperSettings(@NotNull DumperSettings dumperSettings)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by setSettings(Settings...) and subject for removal
      Associates new dumper settings.
      Parameters:
      dumperSettings - the new dumper settings

    • setGeneralSettings

      public void setGeneralSettings(@NotNull GeneralSettings generalSettings)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by setSettings(Settings...) and subject for removal
      Associates new general settings.

      WARNING!

      • Never change the key format! Such attempts will result in an IllegalArgumentException.
      • If the default list, map or set was changed, already existing instances will not be changed. Reload to take effect.
      Parameters:
      generalSettings - the new general settings

    • setUpdaterSettings

      public void setUpdaterSettings(@NotNull UpdaterSettings updaterSettings)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by setSettings(Settings...) and subject for removal
      Associates new updater settings.
      Parameters:
      updaterSettings - the new updater settings

    • getDefaults

      @Nullable public YamlDocument getDefaults()
      Returns the defaults associated with the document, if any were given to YamlDocument.create() method.

      If there are no defaults, returns null.

      Overrides:
      getDefaults in class Section
      Returns:
      the associated defaults
      See Also:
      • Section.hasDefaults()

    • getGeneralSettings

      @NotNull public GeneralSettings getGeneralSettings()
      Returns the general settings associated with the file.
      Returns:
      the associated general settings

      @NotNull public DumperSettings getDumperSettings()
      Returns the dumper settings associated with the file.
      Returns:
      the associated dumper settings

      @NotNull public UpdaterSettings getUpdaterSettings()
      Returns the updater settings associated with the file.
      Returns:
      the associated updater settings

    • getLoaderSettings

      @NotNull public LoaderSettings getLoaderSettings()
      Returns the loader settings associated with the file.
      Returns:
      the associated loader settings

    • getFile

      @Nullable public File getFile()
      Returns the file associated with this document, if any were given to YamlDocument.create() method.

      If there are no defaults, returns null.

      Returns:
      the associated file

    • isRoot

      public boolean isRoot()
      Description copied from class: Section
      Returns whether this section is simultaneously the root section (file).
      Overrides:
      isRoot in class Section
      Returns:
      if this section is the root section

    • create

      public static YamlDocument create(@NotNull File document, @NotNull InputStream defaults, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given file and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      If the given File does not exist, the document will be loaded from a copy of the defaults. If enabled, the file will automatically be created.

      Parameters:
      document - document (does not need to exist)
      defaults - defaults
      settings - settings
      Returns:
      the created and loaded document
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • create

      public static YamlDocument create(@NotNull InputStream document, @NotNull InputStream defaults, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given stream and loads the defaults (another YAML document, later accessible via getDefaults()) from the defaults stream.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      Please note that methods without an I/O parameter will not be usable. Refer to the method documentation for more information.

      Parameters:
      document - document
      defaults - defaults
      settings - settings
      Returns:
      the created and loaded document
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • create

      public static YamlDocument create(@NotNull File document, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given file. The returned document will not have any defaults.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      If the given File does not exist, the document will be loaded from a copy of the defaults. If enabled, the file will automatically be created.

      Parameters:
      document - document (does not need to exist)
      settings - settings
      Returns:
      the created and loaded document
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error

    • create

      public static YamlDocument create(@NotNull InputStream document, @NotNull Settings... settings) throws IOException
      Creates and loads a YAML document from the given stream. The returned document will not have any defaults.

      The provided settings will be stored and used by this document and the defaults. You can overwrite them using setSettings(Settings...)). If settings of any type are not provided, their defaults (e.g. GeneralSettings.DEFAULT) are used.

      If any of the given objects is not an instance of GeneralSettings, LoaderSettings, DumperSettings nor UpdaterSettings, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown. If there are multiple instances of the same settings type, the last one will take effect.

      Please note that methods without an I/O parameter will not be usable. Refer to the method documentation for more information.

      Parameters:
      document - document
      settings - settings
      Returns:
      the created and loaded document
      Throws:
      IOException - an IO error
